(C) According to the question,
B $ R ⇒ B = R
R • N ⇒ R> N
K @ N ⇒ K≤ N

On combining the statements (i), (ii) and (iii), we get
B = R> N≥ K
Conclusions I. B • K ⇒ B> K (true)
II. K % R ⇒ K< R (true)
III. B # N ⇒ B≥ N (false)

So, it is clear that Conclusions I and II are true.

(b) According to the question,
M % R ⇒ M< R
R # T ⇒ R≥ T
T • N ⇒ T> N

On combining the statements (i), (ii) and (iii), we get
M< R≥ T> N
Conclusions I. N % R ⇒ N< R (true)
II. M % T ⇒ M< T (false)
III. M * N ⇒ M> N (false)

So, it is clear that only Conclusion I is true.

(c) Let us check all the options one by one.
From option (a),
5 ÷ 3 × 8 - 12 ÷ 4 = 3
⇒ 5 ÷ 3 × 8 - 12 + 4 = 3
⇒ 5 × 8 - 12 + 4 ÷ 3 ⇒ 40/3 - 12 + 4 ÷ 3
⇒ 40 - 36/3 + 4 ÷ 3 ⇒ 4/3 + 4 ÷ 3 ⇒ LHS ≠ RHS
So, option (a) is incorrect.
From option (b),
5 + 3 × 8 - 12 ÷ 4 = 3
⇒ 5 - 3 × 8 + 12 ÷ 4 = 3 ⇒ 5 - 3 × 8 + 3 = 3
